commit.message:
fix: remove azuread_application data source, use bootstrap_sp_app_object_id variable

data.azuread_application requires Application.Read.All Graph API permission
which sp-crossplane doesn't have — resulted in 403 Forbidden during plan.

Instead, accept the app registration Object ID as an explicit variable
bootstrap_sp_app_object_id and construct the resource ID directly as
/applications/{object_id}. No Graph API read needed.

Changes:
- ado.tf: drop data.azuread_application.sp_crossplane; update federated
  credential application_id to /applications/${var.bootstrap_sp_app_object_id}
- variables.tf: add bootstrap_sp_app_object_id variable
- terraform.tfvars: add Backstage template value placeholder
- tests/test.tfvars: add test value
- tests/*.tftest.hcl: remove now-unused mock_data azuread_application block
- template.yaml: add bootstrap_sp_app_object_id field (required, UUID pattern)
  in Azure Configuration step; wire into fetch:template values

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

array of rulesets. When delete-unmanaged-rulesets is enabled, all

Each ruleset lives in its own JSON file. The action input accepts
comma-separated paths; the orgs.yml supports a YAML array or
comma-separated string. syncOrgRulesets() now takes an array of
file paths instead of a single file that held an array.

* fix: address CCR feedback on rulesets sync

- Use octokit.paginate for rulesets list endpoint to handle pagination
- Replace JSON.stringify comparison with deepEqual for key-order safety
- Validate delete-unmanaged-rulesets type with warning (matching properties)
- Track hasFailed in syncOrgRulesets and propagate to caller
- Update test mocks to use mockPaginate for list endpoints

* test: assert paginate is called for rulesets list endpoint

* perf: set per_page: 100 for rulesets pagination

Reduces API calls from 3 pages to 1 for orgs with up to 75 rulesets.

* fix: address CCR feedback (round 2)

- Whitelist supported fields for PUT/POST requests to prevent 422s
  from read-only fields in exported rulesets
- Allow empty YAML array for rulesets-file to disable inherited rulesets
- Remove unused ruleset-unchanged label from SYNC_KIND_LABELS
- Update README tip to note read-only fields are auto-stripped

* fix: handle ruleset detail fetch errors and duplicate names

- Wrap GET /orgs/{org}/rulesets/{id} in try/catch with warning + continue
- Detect duplicate ruleset names across files and fail fast

* refactor: switch from field whitelist to blocklist for rulesets

Use a blocklist of known read-only fields (id, node_id, source, etc.)
instead of a whitelist. This is future-proof — new fields GitHub adds
are passed through without requiring an action update.

* fix: use blocklist for ruleset comparison too

Use stripRulesetReadonlyFields for both sides of the comparison so

fix: CssStrategy::Module partial rendering for SPA navigation (#212)

* feat: fix CssStrategy::Module partial rendering for SPA navigation

After commit d690975 removed <script> wrappers from compiled templates,
CssStrategy::Module broke during SPA navigation. This commit fixes the
full rendering pipeline and hardens the inventory system.

## What changed

### Partial response contract (handler + router)

render_partial() now emits two separate arrays in the JSON response:
- templateStyles[]: module CSS definition tags for inventory-new components
- templates[]: clean JS IIFE payloads (no hybrid <style>...</style>IIFE strings)

The router appends all templateStyles to <head> first (deduped by specifier),
then executes all template IIFEs in one nonce-friendly <script> tag.
Link/Style modes are unaffected (templateStyles is empty).

### SSR module style emission (handler)

Moved <style type="module"> emission from inline during component rendering
(emit_css_module) to the head_end hook, which now emits definitions for ALL
inventoried components. This ensures the inventory contract holds: when the
inventory says "you have mp-cart-panel", the CSS definition actually exists
in the document for the framework to create CSSStyleSheets from.

The inline emit_css_module() function was removed entirely. This also
eliminates the duplicate "import map rule removed" browser warning that
occurred when both inline and head_end emission produced the same tag.

### Duplicate template override (server.rs + CLI serve)

Both serve_request() and the CLI dev server were calling render_partial()
then overwriting templates/inventory with their own derivation, which
discarded templateStyles entirely. Both now return render_partial() output
directly.

### Framework adoptedStyleSheets dedup (styles.ts)

injectModuleStyle() had a page-level Set that prevented the same specifier
from being processed more than once. After the first shadow root adopted a
stylesheet, all subsequent roots were skipped. Replaced with a Map<string,
CSSStyleSheet> cache: the sheet is parsed once per specifier but adopted
onto every new shadow root that needs it.

### Inventory hash collision (route_handler.rs)

filter_needed_components() was checking the accumulating inventory (built
during iteration) instead of the client's original inventory. When two
component names hash to the same FNV-1a mod 256 bit position (e.g. mp-app
and mp-cart-panel both map to bit 218), the second component was silently
dropped. Fixed to check against the immutable client inventory only.

### Dead code removal

Removed get_route_templates(), get_route_templates_for_request(), and
prepend_css_module() which produced the broken hybrid format and were only
called by their own tests. Removed their re-exports from the webui crate.

### Docker dual-process deployment (commerce)

Added docker-entrypoint.sh and updated Dockerfile to run two server
processes in one container: port 3004 (--css=link) and port 3003
(--css=module).

## Test coverage

- Handler: Style/Link/Module SSR emission, hash collision resilience,
  non-route sibling inclusion, render_partial style/template separation
- Parser: shadow DOM shell fragment graph includes child components
- Server helper: Link/Style/Module partial response shape
- Router: module style ordering + nonce, empty templateStyles for Link/Style
- Framework: 5 new unit tests for injectModuleStyle sheetCache/adoptedStyleSheets
- Commerce: module-mode about page includes cart-panel style in <head>,
  module-mode partial splits styles from templates

* Add comprehensive tests for alternation leftmost-match position bug

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix alternation leftmost-match bug by skipping DFA sandwich

The DFA sandwich (forward DFA → reverse DFA → anchored forward DFA)
incorrectly identifies the match start for alternation patterns when
alternatives match at different positions with different endpoints.
The forward DFA with longest=false returns the earliest match END,
which can come from a later-starting alternative that ends sooner,
causing the reverse DFA to miss the leftmost-starting match.

For example, (bcd|abcde) on text containing 'abcde': the forward DFA
returns end=4 from the 'bcd' match (starting at 1), missing the
leftmost 'abcde' match starting at 0 (which ends at 5).

Fix: mark dfaStartReliable() as false for alternation patterns, so
the DFA sandwich is skipped and the BitState/NFA engine handles the
search. The forward DFA existence check still runs for the fast
negative path.

Fixes #150

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
add read_all_pvts: parse all NAV-PVTs from each FIFO drain

Previously read_pvt() drained up to 512 bytes from the FIFO and returned
only the first NAV-PVT found, silently discarding the rest (up to 4 more
per 512-byte read at 100 bytes/PVT). This caused significant sample loss
whenever the FIFO accumulated multiple packets — e.g. during a 16-second
Notecard transmission at 25 Hz GPS: 40 PVTs queued in chip FIFO, but only
~8 were extracted (1 per 512-byte drain × 8 reads).

Changes:
- Refactor parse_nav_pvt into iter_nav_pvts(buf, &mut f) that calls f()
  for EVERY valid NAV-PVT found in the buffer.  parse_nav_pvt is now a
  thin wrapper (behaviorally unchanged).
- Add MaxM10S::read_all_pvts(i2c, &mut f): drains one chunk and invokes
  f for every packet.  Returns Ok(true) if FIFO had data, Ok(false) when
  empty.  Caller loops until false to fully drain the FIFO.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
